name: Martignas and Les Sablières Loop
description: Explore the moderate 19.6-mile Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle mountain bike route, featuring technical singletrack and scenic river crossings
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Martignas-Sur-Jalle traverse a landscape characterized by extensive forested areas and the Jalle river, which flows through the commune. The terrain features undulating paths typical of the Gironde region, offering a mix of shaded routes and varied surfaces. This area provides access to the vast pine forests of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Reserve, ensuring diverse natural environments for mountain biking. The trails range from easy loops along riverbanks to more challenging paths with roots and sandy sections. Best mountain bike trails around Martignas-Sur-Jalle The most popular mountain bike trail is Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local forests and countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Wooden bridge – VTT en jalles loop from Martignas-sur-Jalle, a difficult 17.5 miles (28.1 km) path. This route features varied terrain, including sections near the Jalle river and a wooden bridge crossing. Local mountain bikers also love the A fun, accessible ride – VTT en jalles loop from Martignas-sur-Jalle, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through forested areas and along the Jalle, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Martignas-Sur-Jalle is defined by riverine landscapes, extensive pine forests, and undulating paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Martignas-Sur-Jalle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1,400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Martignas-Sur-Jalle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Martignas-Sur-Jalle?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Martignas-Sur-Jalle, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy loops to more challenging paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Martignas-Sur-Jalle?
A: The terrain around Martignas-Sur-Jalle is characterized by extensive forested areas and the Jalle river. You can expect undulating paths typical of the Gironde region, with a mix of shaded routes and varied surfaces. Some trails may include sandy sections, roots, and small obstacles, particularly in the vast pine forests of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Reserve.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Martignas-Sur-Jalle?
A: Yes, Martignas-Sur-Jalle offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or family outings. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40079947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A fun, accessible ride – VTT en jalles loop from Martignas-sur-Jalle&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.2-mile route leading through forested areas and along the Jalle river.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28865231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden bridge – VTT en jalles loop from Martignas-sur-Jalle&lt;/a&gt; offer difficult terrain over 17.5 miles, featuring varied surfaces and sections near the Jalle river.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking?
A: While mountain biking, you'll frequently encounter the Jalle river, a designated Natura 2000 protected site known for its diverse flora and fauna. The trails also traverse extensive pine forests, part of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Reserve. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5834204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Footbridge at the Bois des Sources du Peugue&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5184863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majolan Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Martignas-Sur-Jalle suitable for dogs?
A: While the region is generally dog-friendly, especially in natural areas like the Jalle riverbanks and forests, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on the mountain bike trails themselves.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Martignas-Sur-Jalle?
A: The mountain bike trails in Martignas-Sur-Jalle are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse natural environments, the mix of shaded forest paths, and the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels, from easy riverbank loops to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Martignas-Sur-Jalle are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995967174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37620090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;VTT en jalles loop from Martignas-sur-Jalle&lt;/a&gt;, which explore the local forests and countryside.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Martignas-Sur-Jalle?
A: Many trails in Martignas-Sur-Jalle, particularly those along the Jalle river, have convenient starting points with parking. The Moulin Bidon site, for instance, is a popular starting point for outdoor activities and offers amenities including a picnic area, making it a good option for parking before hitting the trails.

Q: Is there public transport access to the mountain bike trails?
A: Martignas-Sur-Jalle is served by local public transport, which can provide access to the town. However, direct public transport links to specific trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus routes and schedules to see if they align with your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Martignas-Sur-Jalle?
A: The region is generally pleasant for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, but the extensive shaded forest paths provide relief. Winter rides are also possible, though some trails might be muddier after rain.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance rides?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41578226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Singletrack Trail – VTT en jalles loop from Martignas-sur-Jalle&lt;/a&gt; cover over 21 miles (34.7 km), offering an extended exploration of the area's diverse landscapes.
tips: Single under the cool of the trees, a bit technical because of the sand in places but no steep drop!
Near Martignas, a nice place to have fun between singles and paths and learn to ride on the sand!

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The most common starting point and access for this route is the Moulin Bidon picnic area. You can typically find parking there.

faqs.question: Is the Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally not considered suitable for absolute beginners. It's rated as moderate and requires advanced riding skills, aligning with an STS difficulty of approximately S2. Expect technical sections, especially sandy singletrack.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll ride on unpaved sections, natural paths, paved roads, gravel, and asphalt. There are significant portions of singletrack (about 10 km), which can be technical due to sand, as well as wider forest tracks. Some parts may also be stony or have roots. Be aware that after heavy rain, sections can become muddy or even impassable due to overflowing marshes.

faqs.question: What are the scenic highlights along the Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle?
faqs.answer: The trail offers pleasant scenery as it frequently borders the Jalle, a small stream, providing views and the sounds of water and birdsong. You'll pass through forests and pine forests, featuring woods, streams, clearings, and marshlands. Notable highlights include the Singletrack of Martignas-sur-Jalle and the Les Sablières area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle is approximately 31 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 25 minutes to complete on a mountain bike, depending on your pace and skill level.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring is particularly pleasant, offering vibrant scenery and birdsong. However, it's best to avoid riding after significant rainfall, as parts of the trail can become very muddy or even impassable due to overflowing marshes and fallen trees.

faqs.question: Is the Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, generally, dogs are allowed on forest trails in this region. It is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Boucle de Martignas-sur-Jalle trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly passes through sections of the ReVE 11 - Bordeaux - Martignas sur Jalles and the Chemin de lutte feux de forêts.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for riding this trail?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for technical sections, especially the sandy singletrack, which can be challenging. After heavy rain, certain areas can become very muddy or even impassable, so checking recent conditions is advisable. Always be aware of potential fallen trees on the path.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Given that the trail requires advanced riding skills and is not suitable for absolute beginners, it is generally not recommended for families with young children or inexperienced riders. More experienced families might enjoy it, but be aware of the technical sections.
